title: The Modes of Nature | HG Amitasana Dasa
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: So, Krishna says,
We are his amshas, but under the modes of material nature, we have got conditioned and living a very, very abominable life.
So, when we are, example is given just like a spark, when it separates from the fire, if it falls on dry grass, there is a possibility that it can again catch fire.
The dry grass is conducive for the spark to ignite fire.
So, likewise, when the living entity is conditioned by the modes of material nature,
but predominantly nature,
when we are in sattva guna, the material energy acts through these modes.
Sometimes sattva guna is predominant, sometimes raja guna is predominant, sometimes tamo guna is predominant.
So, conditioned by sattva guna is like the spark falling on a dry grass.
There is a good possibility that it will ignite the fire again.
The spark becomes fire.
So, likewise, when we are predominantly in sattva guna,
then we can cultivate devotional service, remembrance of the Supreme Lord, cultivate knowledge,
and again revive our original consciousness, which is Krishna consciousness.
When that spark falls on a wet grass, it very soon gets extinguished.
It does not ignite that wet grass.
So, likewise, when the living entity, that amsha of the Lord,
is predominantly under the influence of raja guna,
then that original consciousness gets extinguished.
So, in Kali Yuga, predominantly people are under the influence of raja guna and tamo guna.
And that's why Krishna consciousness activities have no relevance for them.
It doesn't make sense.
If you invite somebody for a Bhagavad Gita lecture, it doesn't make sense.
Invite for a movie, invite for a cricket match, invite for IPL, people queue up.
Makes sense.
And then, if that same spark falls on water, it gets completely extinguished.
So, likewise, when we are predominantly in sattva guna,
then tamo guna, then whatever little bit we have consciousness of the Lord is completely extinguished.
And conditional life in its full force becomes, so to say, comes about.
Full force of conditional life acts on the jiva.
Hare Krishna!
